subject: Counterfactual immunity: recursive improvement selected by tests that can feel a wound

spirals.measure: A pre-registered lesion is bound to a registered suite, applied only to a disposable copy, and retained with baseline/mutant exit codes and exact source bytes.
Syntax-invalid mutants are MUTANT-INVALID rather than KILLED; the runner preserves byte-level newline grammar and clears generated Python caches between baseline and mutant.
A first SURVIVED market-proof lesion created a direct regression test and then KILLED on replay. Three market lesions are current KILLED outcomes and expire under drift when station.py changes.

current_truth: Counterfactual immunity establishes only that the registered station suite distinguishes three exact nearby market lesions. It does not establish complete coverage, general safety, customer demand, or a discovery result.
next_kill: If any current lesion survives, if a stale source passes immunity verification, or if an invalid mutant counts as KILLED, replay and repair before treating the guard as protective.
